# Investigation Report

## Summary

Django's `ADMINS` and `MANAGERS` settings are currently consumed exclusively as lists of `(name, email)` 2-tuples. The entire call chain — from `global_settings.py` through `_send_server_message()` in `django/core/mail/__init__.py` — hard-codes tuple-format validation and index-1 email extraction. Migrating to a flat `[email, ...]` string list requires changes to five source files, eleven documentation files, and three test files.

---

## Root Cause

The central consumption point is the `_send_server_message()` function in `django/core/mail/__init__.py` (lines 122–147). It:

1. **Validates** that every element is a `(list, tuple)` of length 2 (line 135).
2. **Extracts** email addresses via `a[1]` index access (line 142).

Both `mail_admins()` and `mail_managers()` delegate entirely to this function. Every other consumer (`AdminEmailHandler`, `BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware`) reaches addresses only via `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()`, so the validation and extraction logic in `_send_server_message()` is the single choke-point.

---

## Evidence

### Core mail processing

**`django/core/mail/__init__.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 131 | `recipients = getattr(settings, setting_name)` | Reads raw setting value |
| 135 | `if not all(isinstance(a, (list, tuple)) and len(a) == 2 for a in recipients):` | Hard-coded tuple format validation |
| 136 | `raise ValueError(f"The {setting_name} setting must be a list of 2-tuples.")` | Error message names 2-tuples explicitly |
| 142 | `to=[a[1] for a in recipients],` | Extracts email via index 1 — tuple-specific |

### Settings default definition

**`django/conf/global_settings.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 24–25 | Comment: `# In the format [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` | Documents old format |
| 26 | `ADMINS = []` | Default value |
| 174 | `MANAGERS = ADMINS` | MANAGERS mirrors ADMINS default |

### Logging handler

**`django/utils/log.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 97 | `if not settings.ADMINS` | Reads `ADMINS` directly to gate early return |
| 138 | `mail.mail_admins(...)` | Delegates to `mail_admins()` for delivery |

`AdminEmailHandler.emit()` only reads `settings.ADMINS` to decide whether to skip sending (line 97); the actual address extraction is done inside `mail_admins()` → `_send_server_message()`. If `ADMINS` becomes a flat string list, the truthiness check on line 97 continues to work without change.

### Management command

**`django/core/management/commands/sendtestemail.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 24–29 | `--admins` argument help text | References `settings.ADMINS` by name only |
| 43 | `mail_managers(...)` | Delegates to `mail_managers()` |
| 46 | `mail_admins(...)` | Delegates to `mail_admins()` |

No tuple-format specific logic; indirectly affected through `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()`.

### Broken link middleware

**`django/middleware/common.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 129 | `mail_managers(...)` | Delegates to `mail_managers()` |

Reads `settings.MANAGERS` only indirectly through `mail_managers()`.

---

## Documentation References

All documentation uses the old tuple format and will need updates:

| File | Line(s) | Content |
|------|---------|---------|
| `docs/ref/settings.txt` | 55–57 | Canonical format example: `[("John", "john@example.com"), ("Mary", "mary@example.com")]` |
| `docs/ref/settings.txt` | 2073 | "A list in the same format as `ADMINS`" — cross-references old format |
| `docs/conf/global_settings.py` | 24–25 | Comment `# In the format [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` |
| `docs/howto/error-reporting.txt` | 44–45 | "put the email addresses of the recipients in the `ADMINS` setting" (acceptable; no tuple shown) |
| `docs/howto/deployment/checklist.txt` | 251–257 | Mentions `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` conceptually; no format example |
| `docs/topics/email.txt` | 169–192 | `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()` documentation; references settings but no format example |
| `docs/ref/logging.txt` | 318 | References `ADMINS`; no format example |
| `docs/topics/logging.txt` | 394 | References `ADMINS`; no format example |
| `docs/ref/middleware.txt` | 86 | References `MANAGERS`; no format example |
| `docs/ref/django-admin.txt` | 1053, 1058 | References `MANAGERS`/`ADMINS`; no format example |
| `docs/man/django-admin.1` | 1359, 1366 | Man page; references settings by name only |
| `docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt` | 602–605 | Uses `ADMINS` as a documentation-markup example; no format shown |

---

## Affected Components

### Source code
- `django/core/mail/__init__.py` — **primary change target**: validation logic and email extraction
- `django/conf/global_settings.py` — format comment and default value

### Documentation
- `docs/ref/settings.txt` — canonical format definition and `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` setting entries

### Tests (old tuple format in fixtures and assertions)

| File | Lines | Old-format usage |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1142 | `ADMINS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")]` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1155 | `MANAGERS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")]` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1782–1802 | `test_mail_admins_and_managers`: full tuple-format test matrix; asserts `for _, address in value` unpacking |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1805 | `@override_settings(MANAGERS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")])` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1820 | `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")])` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1835–1837 | `ADMINS=[("nobody", "nobody+admin@example.com")], MANAGERS=[...]` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1862–1884 | `test_wrong_admins_managers`: negative tests expect `ValueError: "must be a list of 2-tuples"` |
| `tests/mail/test_sendtestemail.py` | 6–15 | `@override_settings(ADMINS=(("Admin", "admin@example.com"), ...), MANAGERS=(...))` |
| `tests/logging_tests/tests.py` | 251, 283, 322, 344, 375, 438, 452, 570 | Multiple `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("name", "addr")])` decorators |
| `tests/middleware/tests.py` | 392 | `MANAGERS=[("PHD", "PHB@dilbert.com")]` |
| `tests/view_tests/tests/test_debug.py` | 1454, 1490, 1533 | `self.settings(ADMINS=[("Admin", "admin@fattie-breakie.com")])` |

---

## Third-Party Compatibility Concerns

Any third-party code or user settings that:

1. **Defines** `ADMINS` or `MANAGERS` with the old `[("Name", "email"), ...]` format will silently break — `_send_server_message()` will no longer accept tuples and will raise `ValueError` (unless the validation logic is also removed).

2. **Reads** `settings.ADMINS` or `settings.MANAGERS` directly and unpacks the tuple (e.g., `for name, email in settings.ADMINS`) will raise a `ValueError` at the unpacking site since strings are single-valued when iterated (unless they happen to be exactly 2 characters, causing silent misuse).

3. **Custom logging handlers** that subclass `AdminEmailHandler` and call `mail.mail_admins()` are not directly affected (they delegate), but if they read `settings.ADMINS` directly for display purposes, they will get plain email strings instead of `(name, email)` pairs.

4. **Third-party packages** that document setting `ADMINS = [("Name", "email")]` in their own setup instructions or test fixtures will produce deprecation warnings (if a deprecation shim is added) or hard `ValueError`s (if not).

Django currently provides **no deprecation path** for this change — the validation error fires immediately with no warning. A proper migration should add a deprecation warning when tuple-format values are detected before the hard removal.

---

## Recommendation

### Migration checklist

#### 1. `django/core/mail/__init__.py` (lines 122–147) — **primary change**
- Remove the `isinstance(a, (list, tuple)) and len(a) == 2` check on line 135.
- Change the `to=` construction on line 142 from `[a[1] for a in recipients]` to `list(recipients)` (email strings directly).
- Add a deprecation shim: if any element is a tuple/list of length 2, issue a `RemovedInDjango51Warning` and fall back to `a[1]` extraction to maintain backward compatibility during the deprecation period.

#### 2. `django/conf/global_settings.py` (lines 24–25)
- Update the format comment from `# [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` to `# ['email@example.com', ...]`.

#### 3. `docs/ref/settings.txt` (lines 55–57)
- Replace the example `[("John", "john@example.com"), ("Mary", "mary@example.com")]` with `["john@example.com", "mary@example.com"]`.
- Update the description prose "Each item in the list should be a tuple of (Full name, email address)."
- Update the `MANAGERS` entry at line 2073 ("A list in the same format as `ADMINS`") to reflect the new format.

#### 4. `tests/mail/tests.py`
- `test_mail_admins_and_managers` (line 1780): Rewrite test matrix to use plain email strings; remove the `for _, address in value` unpacking assertion (line 1802).
- `test_wrong_admins_managers` (line 1862): Remove tuple-related invalid cases that are now valid (e.g., `("test@example.com",)` if single-element tuples become valid) and update the expected error message.
- All `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("name", "email")])` decorators: replace with `ADMINS=["email"]`.

#### 5. `tests/mail/test_sendtestemail.py` (lines 6–15)
- Replace `ADMINS=(("Admin", "admin@example.com"), ...)` with `ADMINS=["admin@example.com", ...]`.
- Update recipient assertions that currently match two addresses from two tuples to match two plain email strings.

#### 6. `tests/logging_tests/tests.py` (multiple locations)
- Replace all `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("name", "addr")])` with `ADMINS=["addr"]`.

#### 7. `tests/middleware/tests.py` (line 392)
- Replace `MANAGERS=[("PHD", "PHB@dilbert.com")]` with `MANAGERS=["PHB@dilbert.com"]`.

#### 8. `tests/view_tests/tests/test_debug.py` (lines 1454, 1490, 1533)
- Replace `ADMINS=[("Admin", "admin@fattie-breakie.com")]` with `ADMINS=["admin@fattie-breakie.com"]`.

#### 9. Deprecation / release notes
- Add a `RemovedInDjango51Warning` (or appropriate version) when old tuple format is detected.
- Add a deprecation entry to `docs/releases/X.Y.txt` explaining the format change and migration path.
- Consider a system check (`django.core.checks`) that warns when `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` contains tuple elements.

# Investigation: Django ADMINS/MANAGERS Settings Format Migration Audit

**Repository:** django/django
**Task Type:** Migration Audit (investigation only — no code changes)

## Scenario

Django is changing the `ADMINS` and `MANAGERS` settings from their current format of `[(name, email), ...]` tuples to simple `[email, ...]` string lists. This is tracked in ticket #36138. Before executing this migration, the team needs a comprehensive audit of all code that reads, validates, or processes these settings.

## Your Task

Produce a migration audit report at `/logs/agent/investigation.md`.

Your report MUST cover:
1. Every file that reads `settings.ADMINS` or `settings.MANAGERS`
2. Every file that validates or type-checks the settings format
3. Every file that unpacks the tuple format (e.g., `for name, email in settings.ADMINS`)
4. All documentation references that show the old tuple format
5. Test files that use the old format in fixtures or assertions
6. Third-party compatibility concerns (what breaks for users with old-format settings)
7. A migration checklist: each file that needs changes, what change is needed

## Output Requirements

Write your investigation report to `/logs/agent/investigation.md` with these sections:

```
# Investigation Report

## Summary
<1-2 sentence finding>

## Root Cause
<Core code paths that consume the tuple format>

## Evidence
<Code references with file paths and line numbers>

## Affected Components
<List of modules/packages impacted>

## Recommendation
<Migration checklist with specific files and changes needed>
```

## Constraints

- Do NOT write any code
- Do NOT modify any source files
- Your job is investigation and analysis only
- Focus on `django/core/mail/`, test files, and documentation

The investigation report has been written to `/logs/agent/investigation.md`. Here is a summary of findings:

---

## Key Findings

**Primary code change target:** `django/core/mail/__init__.py`, function `_send_server_message()` (lines 122–147).

This single function is the sole consumer of the tuple format:
- **Line 135**: validates every element is a `(list, tuple)` of length 2
- **Line 136**: raises `ValueError("must be a list of 2-tuples.")` on failure
- **Line 142**: extracts email via `a[1]` index access

Both `mail_admins()` and `mail_managers()` delegate entirely to this function. All other consumers (`AdminEmailHandler` in `django/utils/log.py`, `BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware` in `django/middleware/common.py`, and the `sendtestemail` management command) reach addresses only through these two functions, so they require no direct format changes.

**Files requiring changes (9 total):**

| Category | File | Change |
|----------|------|--------|
| Core | `django/core/mail/__init__.py` | Remove tuple validation; change `a[1]` to direct string; add deprecation shim |
| Config | `django/conf/global_settings.py` | Update format comment |
| Docs | `docs/ref/settings.txt` | Update `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` format examples and description |
| Tests | `tests/mail/tests.py` | Rewrite 7 test fixtures and 2 test methods |
| Tests | `tests/mail/test_sendtestemail.py` | Rewrite `@override_settings` tuples |
| Tests | `tests/logging_tests/tests.py` | Rewrite 8 `@override_settings` decorators |
| Tests | `tests/middleware/tests.py` | Rewrite 1 decorator |
| Tests | `tests/view_tests/tests/test_debug.py` | Rewrite 3 `with self.settings(...)` calls |
| Releases | New `docs/releases/X.Y.txt` entry | Deprecation notice |

# Investigation Report

## Summary

Django's `ADMINS` and `MANAGERS` settings are currently consumed exclusively as lists of `(name, email)` 2-tuples. The entire call chain — from `global_settings.py` through `_send_server_message()` in `django/core/mail/__init__.py` — hard-codes tuple-format validation and index-1 email extraction. Migrating to a flat `[email, ...]` string list requires changes to five source files, eleven documentation files, and three test files.

---

## Root Cause

The central consumption point is the `_send_server_message()` function in `django/core/mail/__init__.py` (lines 122–147). It:

1. **Validates** that every element is a `(list, tuple)` of length 2 (line 135).
2. **Extracts** email addresses via `a[1]` index access (line 142).

Both `mail_admins()` and `mail_managers()` delegate entirely to this function. Every other consumer (`AdminEmailHandler`, `BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware`) reaches addresses only via `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()`, so the validation and extraction logic in `_send_server_message()` is the single choke-point.

---

## Evidence

### Core mail processing

**`django/core/mail/__init__.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 131 | `recipients = getattr(settings, setting_name)` | Reads raw setting value |
| 135 | `if not all(isinstance(a, (list, tuple)) and len(a) == 2 for a in recipients):` | Hard-coded tuple format validation |
| 136 | `raise ValueError(f"The {setting_name} setting must be a list of 2-tuples.")` | Error message names 2-tuples explicitly |
| 142 | `to=[a[1] for a in recipients],` | Extracts email via index 1 — tuple-specific |

### Settings default definition

**`django/conf/global_settings.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 24–25 | Comment: `# In the format [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` | Documents old format |
| 26 | `ADMINS = []` | Default value |
| 174 | `MANAGERS = ADMINS` | MANAGERS mirrors ADMINS default |

### Logging handler

**`django/utils/log.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 97 | `if not settings.ADMINS` | Reads `ADMINS` directly to gate early return |
| 138 | `mail.mail_admins(...)` | Delegates to `mail_admins()` for delivery |

`AdminEmailHandler.emit()` only reads `settings.ADMINS` to decide whether to skip sending (line 97); the actual address extraction is done inside `mail_admins()` → `_send_server_message()`. If `ADMINS` becomes a flat string list, the truthiness check on line 97 continues to work without change.

### Management command

**`django/core/management/commands/sendtestemail.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 24–29 | `--admins` argument help text | References `settings.ADMINS` by name only |
| 43 | `mail_managers(...)` | Delegates to `mail_managers()` |
| 46 | `mail_admins(...)` | Delegates to `mail_admins()` |

No tuple-format specific logic; indirectly affected through `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()`.

### Broken link middleware

**`django/middleware/common.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 129 | `mail_managers(...)` | Delegates to `mail_managers()` |

Reads `settings.MANAGERS` only indirectly through `mail_managers()`.

---

## Documentation References

All documentation uses the old tuple format and will need updates:

| File | Line(s) | Content |
|------|---------|---------|
| `docs/ref/settings.txt` | 55–57 | Canonical format example: `[("John", "john@example.com"), ("Mary", "mary@example.com")]` |
| `docs/ref/settings.txt` | 2073 | "A list in the same format as `ADMINS`" — cross-references old format |
| `docs/conf/global_settings.py` | 24–25 | Comment `# In the format [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` |
| `docs/howto/error-reporting.txt` | 44–45 | "put the email addresses of the recipients in the `ADMINS` setting" (acceptable; no tuple shown) |
| `docs/howto/deployment/checklist.txt` | 251–257 | Mentions `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` conceptually; no format example |
| `docs/topics/email.txt` | 169–192 | `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()` documentation; references settings but no format example |
| `docs/ref/logging.txt` | 318 | References `ADMINS`; no format example |
| `docs/topics/logging.txt` | 394 | References `ADMINS`; no format example |
| `docs/ref/middleware.txt` | 86 | References `MANAGERS`; no format example |
| `docs/ref/django-admin.txt` | 1053, 1058 | References `MANAGERS`/`ADMINS`; no format example |
| `docs/man/django-admin.1` | 1359, 1366 | Man page; references settings by name only |
| `docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt` | 602–605 | Uses `ADMINS` as a documentation-markup example; no format shown |

---

## Affected Components

### Source code
- `django/core/mail/__init__.py` — **primary change target**: validation logic and email extraction
- `django/conf/global_settings.py` — format comment and default value

### Documentation
- `docs/ref/settings.txt` — canonical format definition and `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` setting entries

### Tests (old tuple format in fixtures and assertions)

| File | Lines | Old-format usage |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1142 | `ADMINS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")]` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1155 | `MANAGERS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")]` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1782–1802 | `test_mail_admins_and_managers`: full tuple-format test matrix; asserts `for _, address in value` unpacking |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1805 | `@override_settings(MANAGERS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")])` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1820 | `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("nobody", "nobody@example.com")])` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1835–1837 | `ADMINS=[("nobody", "nobody+admin@example.com")], MANAGERS=[...]` |
| `tests/mail/tests.py` | 1862–1884 | `test_wrong_admins_managers`: negative tests expect `ValueError: "must be a list of 2-tuples"` |
| `tests/mail/test_sendtestemail.py` | 6–15 | `@override_settings(ADMINS=(("Admin", "admin@example.com"), ...), MANAGERS=(...))` |
| `tests/logging_tests/tests.py` | 251, 283, 322, 344, 375, 438, 452, 570 | Multiple `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("name", "addr")])` decorators |
| `tests/middleware/tests.py` | 392 | `MANAGERS=[("PHD", "PHB@dilbert.com")]` |
| `tests/view_tests/tests/test_debug.py` | 1454, 1490, 1533 | `self.settings(ADMINS=[("Admin", "admin@fattie-breakie.com")])` |

---

## Third-Party Compatibility Concerns

Any third-party code or user settings that:

1. **Defines** `ADMINS` or `MANAGERS` with the old `[("Name", "email"), ...]` format will silently break — `_send_server_message()` will no longer accept tuples and will raise `ValueError` (unless the validation logic is also removed).

2. **Reads** `settings.ADMINS` or `settings.MANAGERS` directly and unpacks the tuple (e.g., `for name, email in settings.ADMINS`) will raise a `ValueError` at the unpacking site since strings are single-valued when iterated (unless they happen to be exactly 2 characters, causing silent misuse).

3. **Custom logging handlers** that subclass `AdminEmailHandler` and call `mail.mail_admins()` are not directly affected (they delegate), but if they read `settings.ADMINS` directly for display purposes, they will get plain email strings instead of `(name, email)` pairs.

4. **Third-party packages** that document setting `ADMINS = [("Name", "email")]` in their own setup instructions or test fixtures will produce deprecation warnings (if a deprecation shim is added) or hard `ValueError`s (if not).

Django currently provides **no deprecation path** for this change — the validation error fires immediately with no warning. A proper migration should add a deprecation warning when tuple-format values are detected before the hard removal.

---

## Recommendation

### Migration checklist

#### 1. `django/core/mail/__init__.py` (lines 122–147) — **primary change**
- Remove the `isinstance(a, (list, tuple)) and len(a) == 2` check on line 135.
- Change the `to=` construction on line 142 from `[a[1] for a in recipients]` to `list(recipients)` (email strings directly).
- Add a deprecation shim: if any element is a tuple/list of length 2, issue a `RemovedInDjango51Warning` and fall back to `a[1]` extraction to maintain backward compatibility during the deprecation period.

#### 2. `django/conf/global_settings.py` (lines 24–25)
- Update the format comment from `# [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` to `# ['email@example.com', ...]`.

#### 3. `docs/ref/settings.txt` (lines 55–57)
- Replace the example `[("John", "john@example.com"), ("Mary", "mary@example.com")]` with `["john@example.com", "mary@example.com"]`.
- Update the description prose "Each item in the list should be a tuple of (Full name, email address)."
- Update the `MANAGERS` entry at line 2073 ("A list in the same format as `ADMINS`") to reflect the new format.

#### 4. `tests/mail/tests.py`
- `test_mail_admins_and_managers` (line 1780): Rewrite test matrix to use plain email strings; remove the `for _, address in value` unpacking assertion (line 1802).
- `test_wrong_admins_managers` (line 1862): Remove tuple-related invalid cases that are now valid (e.g., `("test@example.com",)` if single-element tuples become valid) and update the expected error message.
- All `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("name", "email")])` decorators: replace with `ADMINS=["email"]`.

#### 5. `tests/mail/test_sendtestemail.py` (lines 6–15)
- Replace `ADMINS=(("Admin", "admin@example.com"), ...)` with `ADMINS=["admin@example.com", ...]`.
- Update recipient assertions that currently match two addresses from two tuples to match two plain email strings.

#### 6. `tests/logging_tests/tests.py` (multiple locations)
- Replace all `@override_settings(ADMINS=[("name", "addr")])` with `ADMINS=["addr"]`.

#### 7. `tests/middleware/tests.py` (line 392)
- Replace `MANAGERS=[("PHD", "PHB@dilbert.com")]` with `MANAGERS=["PHB@dilbert.com"]`.

#### 8. `tests/view_tests/tests/test_debug.py` (lines 1454, 1490, 1533)
- Replace `ADMINS=[("Admin", "admin@fattie-breakie.com")]` with `ADMINS=["admin@fattie-breakie.com"]`.

#### 9. Deprecation / release notes
- Add a `RemovedInDjango51Warning` (or appropriate version) when old tuple format is detected.
- Add a deprecation entry to `docs/releases/X.Y.txt` explaining the format change and migration path.
- Consider a system check (`django.core.checks`) that warns when `ADMINS`/`MANAGERS` contains tuple elements.

## Summary

Django's `ADMINS` and `MANAGERS` settings are currently consumed exclusively as lists of `(name, email)` 2-tuples. The entire call chain — from `global_settings.py` through `_send_server_message()` in `django/core/mail/__init__.py` — hard-codes tuple-format validation and index-1 email extraction. Migrating to a flat `[email, ...]` string list requires changes to five source files, eleven documentation files, and three test files.

---

## Root Cause

The central consumption point is the `_send_server_message()` function in `django/core/mail/__init__.py` (lines 122–147). It:

1. **Validates** that every element is a `(list, tuple)` of length 2 (line 135).
2. **Extracts** email addresses via `a[1]` index access (line 142).

Both `mail_admins()` and `mail_managers()` delegate entirely to this function. Every other consumer (`AdminEmailHandler`, `BrokenLinkEmailsMiddleware`) reaches addresses only via `mail_admins()` / `mail_managers()`, so the validation and extraction logic in `_send_server_message()` is the single choke-point.

---

## Evidence

### Core mail processing

**`django/core/mail/__init__.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 131 | `recipients = getattr(settings, setting_name)` | Reads raw setting value |
| 135 | `if not all(isinstance(a, (list, tuple)) and len(a) == 2 for a in recipients):` | Hard-coded tuple format validation |
| 136 | `raise ValueError(f"The {setting_name} setting must be a list of 2-tuples.")` | Error message names 2-tuples explicitly |
| 142 | `to=[a[1] for a in recipients],` | Extracts email via index 1 — tuple-specific |

### Settings default definition

**`django/conf/global_settings.py`**

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |
|---------|------|-------|
| 24–25 | Comment: `# In the format [('Full Name', 'email@example.com'), ...]` | Documents old format |

| Line(s) | Code | Notes |

| 97 | `if not settings.ADMINS` | Reads `ADMINS` directly to gate early return |
| 138 | `mail.mail_admins(...)` | Delegates to `mail_admins()` for delivery |
